data.title: Invesco raised to Buy-equivalent at RBC Capital Markets
data.description:
Invesco (IVZ) was trading higher after RBC Capital Markets upgraded the investment manager on the back of an improved organic growth outlook and potential incremental margin expansion.

Shares were +1.25% Wednesday pre-market to $28.26.

The investment bank raised its recommendation on the stock to Outperform from Sector Perform, and increased the price target to $35 from $25.

The rating upgrade considers a potential incremental margin expansion to 37%-38% in 2026/2027 due to operating leverage.

RBC Capital Markets factors in potential earnings accretion from the recent QQQ ETF re-classification.

"We are updating our 4Q EPS estimate to $0.58 (from $0.56), partly driven by upwardly revised market return assumptions, refined net flow assumptions as we incorporate some of IVZ's monthly AUM updates, and slight benefit from QQQ ETF re-classification," said analyst Kenneth Lee.

The rating aligns with the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Meanwhile, the Quant Rating system grades the stock as Strong Buy.

data.title: The Ithaka Group adds HWM, AVGO, CRDO and exits BX, ELF, MRVL, TTD
data.description:
• Ithaka Group  underperformed the Russell 1000 Growth Index in Q4 2025, returning a loss of 5.9% gross as compared to a gain of 1.1% for the R1000G.
  • In Q4 2025, the fund added new positions in Howmet (NYSE:HWM), Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO), and Credo (NASDAQ:CRDO).
  • During Q4 2025, the fund exited positions in Blackstone (NYSE:BX), e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF), Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL), and The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD).
  • Source.

data.title: Trump bans large investors from buying homes; to name Fed chair soon
data.description:
President Donald Trump said on Wednesday he signed an executive order to prohibit large institutional investors from buying single-family homes, he said on Wednesday at the World Economic Forum at Davos, Switzerland.

He's also calling on Congress to pass that ban into law, he said. "America will not become a nation of renters."

"I am very protective of people who already own a house," Trump. 

He also instructed government entities (i.e., Fannie Mae (FNMA), Freddie Mac (FMCC)) to buy $200B of mortgage-backed securities to bring mortgage rates down.

Trump also bashed credit card lenders for high interest rates. "Whatever happened to usury?" he asked. Trump said he'll ask Congress to pass a law to cap credit card interest rates at 10% for one year.

The president also said he'll announce a new Fed chairman in the "not too distant future." "He'll be great," he said. "The problem is they change once they get the job."

"We should be paying the lowest interest rate of anybody," he said because of the U.S.'s strong growth.

He sees the Fed raising interest rates as keeping the U.S. from being successful. "Growth doesn't mean inflation… growth can fight inflation, proper growth," he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Abbott Laboratories Q4 2025 Earnings Preview
data.description:
Abbott Laboratories (ABT) is scheduled to announce Q4 earnings results on Thursday, January 22nd, before market open.

The consensus EPS estimate is $1.49 (+11.2% Y/Y) and the consensus revenue estimate is $11.8B (+7.3% Y/Y).

Over the last 2 years, ABT has beaten EPS estimates 88% of the time and has beaten revenue estimates 63% of the time.

Over the last 3 months, EPS estimates have seen 3 upward revisions and 2 downward. Revenue estimates have seen 5 upward revisions and 3 downward.

ABT is rated Hold by Seeking Alpha’s Quant model. The stock demonstrates a mixed performance with stretched valuation, impressive profitability, mixed momentum, and moderate growth, leading to a neutral outlook, the model explains.

Shares of Abbott Laboratories (ABT) have grown 4% over the past year, underperforming the S&P 500's ~13% total return. The 6-month performance shows a decline of ~2%, suggesting recent challenges.

data.title: Stocks open up as Trump calls for Greenland 'negotiations'
data.description:
Stocks opened higher Wednesday after President Donald Trump eased market concerns about a U.S. invasion to secure Greenland.

The S&P 500 (SP500) rose +0.32% while the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJI) advanced +0.32%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) edged up +0.17%.

Trump said at the WEF in Davos that he was seeking immediate negotiations for the acquisition of Greenland from Denmark and doesn't want to use excessive force.

Treasury yields fell across the curve. The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) slipped 1.9 basis points to 4.28%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) declined 1.4 basis points to 3.59%. The 30-year yield (US30Y) eased 1.6 basis points to 4.91%.

Top S&P 500 gainers included Intel (INTC) +5.99%, Teledyne Technologies (TDY) +5.92%, and United Airlines (UAL) +5.18%. Decliners included Kraft Heinz (KHC) -6.61%, Netflix (NFLX) -4.78%, and PTC (PTC) +0.02%.

data.title: Caledonia raises $150M for Zimbabwe gold mine in international capital raise
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (CMCL) said it raised $150M through a seven-year convertible senior notes offering to fund its Bilboes project, which is expected to become Zimbabwe's largest gold mine when it starts operations.

Caledonia's (CMCL) debt issuance is noteworthy as the biggest international capital raising in more than a decade for Zimbabwe, which had been shunned by global investors due to economic and policy volatility.

Caledonia (CMCL), which operates the 80K oz/year Blanket mine in Zimbabwe, said the bond issue is part of a broader strategy it is pursuing to fund Bilboes, which is expected to start production in late 2028 and expected to reach annual output of 200K oz from 2029 for an initial 10-year period.

"Receiving more than $600M of demand from high-quality North American investors is a tremendous endorsement of our strategy, the quality of our assets, our operational track record and the long-term prospects of the company," Caledonia (CMCL) CEO Mark Learmonth said.

data.title: Seagate rises as BNP Paribas upgrades, ups target
data.description:
• Seagate Technology (STX) shares rose 2.9% in premarket trading on Wednesday as BNP Paribas upgraded the storage maker, and upped its price target.
  • “We are now upgrading Seagate from Neutral to Outperform as we are gaining greater conviction that robust data center storage demand could drive an upcycle longer than we initially expected,” analyst Karl Ackerman wrote in a note to clients. “We think a secular re-rating of Seagate (as well as Western Digital) to over 20x is justified.”
  • Ackerman also raised his price target on Seagate to $380, citing the strength in the hard-disk drive market.
  • Delving deeper, Ackerman said he expects the hard-disk drive market to see 25% compound annual growth from now until 2028, due in large part to “rapidly expanding” artificial intelligence infrastructure deployment and AI apps.
  • “We estimate total HDD capacity shipped in CY25 could reach 1.6 ZB and grow mid-20% CAGR to 3.2 ZB by CY28,” Ackerman added. “Nearline HDDs, garnering 75% of all HDD capacity, should grow at 29% CAGR through CY28.”
  • And with the market effectively a duopoly between Seagate and Western Digital (WDC), it's likely there will be continued “disciplined capex investments and long-term profitability,” Ackerman added.

data.title: Déjà Vu at lululemon as Get Low yoga pants pulled over quality concerns
data.description:
Shares of lululemon athletica (LULU) were on the defensive late Tuesday and into Wednesday’s open as a quality control issue with their new Get Low collection led the company to remove the clothing from its website.

The latest development mirrors previous occasions where customers have complained about the quality or design of its popular yoga pants. In July of 2024, shortly after its launch, lululemon (LULU) pulled its Breezethrough collection amid complaints of an unflattering design and poor fit. And in 2013, sales of its Luon yoga pants were also paused due to the transparency of the fabric, leading the company to issue full refunds or exchanges.

Designed with seamless technology to provide a “sculpted look and feel in weightless, fast-drying fabric,” lululemon’s (LULU) Get Low collection was to help reinforce the brand as technically superior to competitors and justify its premium pricing. Instead, customers complained that when bending over or squatting, the fabric becomes see-through.

Consequently, the new line has been temporarily pulled from its website to give the company time to “better understand some initial guest feedback and support with product education.” Get Low will continue to be sold at lululemon’s (LULU) North American stores and online in its overseas markets.

As another issue is raised with the company’s quality control, Wall Street analysts are quick to point out continued stumbles at a company struggling to outmaneuver its competitors.

“Repeated missteps in the core category raise questions around consistency and the strength of LULU's innovation engine," Jefferies’ Randal Konik said, adding that each lapse raises the likelihood of customers going to their competitors like Alo Yoga, Vuori, and NikeSKIMS (NKE), and further "eroding the core.”

“Key risks for LULU shares center on a business whose productivity is deteriorating at the same time the company is stretching itself operationally and strategically,” Konik adds.

data.title: Ionis, Otsuka win EU nod for hereditary angioedema therapy
data.description:
Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS) and its Japanese partner, Otsuka Pharmaceutical (OTSKY), said on Wednesday that the European Commission has approved their RNA-targeted medicine, Dawnzera, as a treatment for hereditary angioedema, a rare genetic disorder.

Therefore, the subcutaneously delivered therapy will be available in the EU for those aged 12 years and older to prevent their recurrent attacks of hereditary angioedema, a disease characterized by severe swelling in various parts of the body.

The approval follows a positive opinion issued by an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency, in November based on data from the companies’ Phase 3 OASIS-HAE and OASISplus studies, in which Dawnzera led to a decline in mean monthly HAE attack rate.

With Otsuka (OTSKF) having licensing rights for the drug in Europe and Asia Pacific, Ionis (IONS) is entitled to a $15M milestone payment and up to 30% in tiered royalties on net product sales from Dawnzera
